fr:title.text: coherent remainder as a path object

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: coherent remainder as a path object

html:li.html:p: We exhibit the latter as a right-cloven family of path objects over the former with the cleaving that for \eta _0,\eta _1\colon g\circ f\sim 1_{A} and \eta _{01}\colon \eta _0 \sim \eta _1 defines \eta _{01}^*\colon \mathsf {act}_{f}\circ \eta _1\sim \epsilon \circ f \to \mathsf {act}_{f}\circ \eta _0\sim \epsilon \circ f as follows:

fr:mainmatter.html:p: With this cleaving in mind, finally define \mathsf {coherentRemainder}_{f}\sigma to be the following contravariant sum of path objects:

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: References
Context

fr:title.text: Coherence of different notions of equivalence › Coherence of inverses

fr:title.text: The coherent remainder of a section

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: The coherent remainder of a section

fr:title.text: coherent remainder as a path object

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: coherent remainder as a path object

html:li.html:p: We exhibit the latter as a right-cloven family of path objects over the former with the cleaving that for \eta _0,\eta _1\colon g\circ f\sim 1_{A} and \eta _{01}\colon \eta _0 \sim \eta _1 defines \eta _{01}^*\colon \mathsf {act}_{f}\circ \eta _1\sim \epsilon \circ f \to \mathsf {act}_{f}\circ \eta _0\sim \epsilon \circ f as follows:

fr:mainmatter.html:p: With this cleaving in mind, finally define \mathsf {coherentRemainder}_{f}\sigma to be the following contravariant sum of path objects:

fr:title.text: The coherent remainder of a section is a proposition

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: The coherent remainder of a section is a proposition

fr:mainmatter.html:p: By Lemma , we may argue that \mathsf {coherentRemainder}_{f}\sigma is a retract of a proposition. We let P be the following type defined using path object structure of fibers given in Example :
It is not difficult to show using Definition that \mathsf {coherentRemainder}_{f}\sigma is a retract of P; we will not need the further fact that it is equivalent to P.

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Being invertible is a proposition

fr:mainmatter.html:p: For any function f\colon A\to B, the type of inverses to f is a proposition.

fr:title.text: Being an embedding is a proposition

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Being an embedding is a proposition

fr:mainmatter.html:p: That a given function f\colon A \to B is an embedding is a proposition.
By Corollary and Corollary .

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Backlinks

fr:title.text: The coherent remainder of a section is a proposition

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: The coherent remainder of a section is a proposition

fr:mainmatter.html:p: By Lemma , we may argue that \mathsf {coherentRemainder}_{f}\sigma is a retract of a proposition. We let P be the following type defined using path object structure of fibers given in Example :
It is not difficult to show using Definition that \mathsf {coherentRemainder}_{f}\sigma is a retract of P; we will not need the further fact that it is equivalent to P.

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Related

fr:title.text: The coherent remainder of a section

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: The coherent remainder of a section

fr:title.text: Contravariant sum of right-cloven families of path objects

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Contravariant sum of right-cloven families of path objects

fr:title.text: Right cleaving of a family of path objects

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Right cleaving of a family of path objects
Dependent product of path objects

fr:mainmatter.html:p: The product of a family \mathcal {B}\colon A \to \mathsf {PathObj} path objects is a path object.
Univalence follows from the closure of families of contractible types under dependent products (Lemma ) via the distributivity of \prod over \sum .

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Path object

fr:mainmatter.html:p: We will write \mathsf {PathObj} for the (large) type of path objects. In our notation, we will at times silently coerce a given path object to its underlying type of vertices.

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Section-retraction pair

fr:title.text: The intrinsic path object on a type

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: The intrinsic path object on a type

fr:mainmatter.html:p: The discrete reflexive graph on a type A is a path object, which we shall refer to as the intrinsic path object on A.
Univalence follows immediately by identification induction.

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Contributions
